title: Snowflake credentials should not be disclosed
description: Snowflake is a cloud data platform used for data warehousing, data lakes, and analytics. Snowflake credentials include account identifiers, usernames, passwords, private keys for key-pair authentication, and OAuth tokens. Exposing these credentials grants unauthorized access to potentially massive datasets and sensitive business intelligence.
recommendation: Remove Snowflake credentials from source code. Use key-pair authentication with private keys stored in a secrets manager. Consider using Snowflake OAuth for application access. Rotate passwords immediately if exposed.
impact: Unauthorized access to data warehouses containing business intelligence, customer data, financial records, and potentially petabytes of sensitive analytical data.

checks.message: Snowflake password detected in variable assignment. Snowflake passwords authenticate users to cloud data warehouses that may contain terabytes of sensitive business data, customer records, financial information, and proprietary analytics. Exposing a Snowflake password allows full data warehouse access including executing arbitrary SQL, exporting entire datasets, and potentially accessing multiple databases. Rotate immediately in Snowflake and enable MFA.

Snowflake connection with hardcoded password detected. Direct connection strings with embedded Snowflake passwords expose complete data warehouse credentials. This grants full access to query, export, and modify all accessible Snowflake databases and schemas. Rotate credentials immediately and migrate to environment variables or key-pair authentication.

Snowflake private key for key-pair authentication detected. Snowflake key-pair authentication uses RSA private keys instead of passwords for programmatic access. Exposing this private key grants the same full data warehouse access as a password, enabling bulk data export, query execution, and administrative operations on your Snowflake account. Remove from source code and store in a secrets manager.

Snowflake OAuth token detected. Snowflake OAuth tokens provide time-limited authenticated access to Snowflake APIs and query execution. Exposing an OAuth token allows unauthorized data warehouse operations during the token's validity period. Revoke via Snowflake session management and rotate OAuth client credentials.
